title: Lovely Smoky Peruvian Beetroot Lomo Saltado
description: A smoky and crumbly Peruvian dish featuring beetroot, katsuramuki-cut, ovo-vegetarian-friendly, in the form of lomo saltado. This recipe combines the flavors of Peru with the sweetness of beetroot.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the katsuramuki-cut beetroot and cook for 5 minutes, until it is tender and lightly browned.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, vinegar, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the sauce over the beetroot mixture and stir to combine.
Push the beetroot mixture to one side of the skillet.
Add a little more oil to the empty side of the skillet, then pour in the beaten eggs.
Scramble the eggs until they are cooked through, then mix them with the beetroot mixture.
Serve the lomo saltado hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
